The potential cost of an unnecessary ER visit (100) far outweighs the cost of a missed emergency (1). Therefore, choosing NO minimizes expected cost.

Given the low cost of a false negative (1) vs. the high cost of a false positive (10), the expected cost strongly favors immediate emergency evaluation.)
